_derivation.purpose: Segmented door-randomizer metadata (BioRand Map.cs subset) — docs/dc1/DOOR-RANDOMIZER-PLAN.md §6.
_derivation.scope: 96 story rooms (stages 1-6). Stage variants 7/8/9 (alt-state of 3/5/6), demo stages A/B/C, and the unused ST60E shell (060E, unfinished room — uncompressed segment, no lighting/enemy triggers/final textures) are excluded by being absent here; resolve by id never name (31 names non-unique).
_derivation.category: Box = room name contains "Storage" (supply/item-box first cut, refine by playtest). Bridge = decoded door out-degree>2 among story rooms. Segment = default (floor grouping from room-data.json.map). All noisy first-cut classifiers per plan §6.
_derivation.floor: Normalized from room-data.json.map (B3/B2/B1/1F/2F/Outdoors; Unknown for the 18 None/2 malformed/2 mixed-floor entries).
_derivation.staticTargets: Per-room one-way decoded door destinations (no reciprocal edge) — excluded from the reciprocal-only shuffle (decision 3). Keyed by destination SSRR, robust across rebuilds (FileOffset is recomputed each load).
_derivation.requires: Graph-logic-parity overlay (GRAPH-LOGIC-PARITY-PLAN.md §4.1), parsed by MapRequirements and stamped onto the room graph; 19 door `requires` + 9 door `requiresRoom` gates authored (recounted 2026-07-13; +4 requiresRoom from the laser-fence state-driver decode — the four residual partitions of STATIC-SCD-RE.md cont.61 §B: 0102→{0107,0101,0104} and 010A→107. A fence wall polls GetFlag(0, op58-type-4 rec+5) (0=beams active/blocking); crossing is passable once the keypad is OPERABLE, i.e. its enable flag GetFlag(0, subtype-11 AOT rec+0x1F, 0x240-0x253) is set by the room's decoded unlock event, so the gate = that event's producer room(s). The other collocated fences add NO new gate: 0108/0502/0608 sit on partitions already gated by the 9:6 story lock / the type-6/type-8 Key Card doors (KeyItemPlacer via the door TYPE byte), 0301/0306 self-enable on arrival, 030A/030D wall sits between the two 030C doors, 0500 init forces its fence down on first visit. STATIC-SCD-RE.md cont.62, ledger rows in placement-gates.md). `requires` PAIRS can also come from a NATIVE gate: extract_logic.py's op58-subtype-3 rule decodes the DDK disc-pair check (Input disc AND Code disc id+7, native comparator 0x4B182A) and emits both ids — STATIC-SCD-RE.md cont.61 §C. Per-room optional keys: "requiresRoom": [SSRR,...] = rooms that must be visited/reachable (AND, applied on entry to this room); "doors": {"<destSSRR>": {"requires": [itemId,...], "requiresRoom": [SSRR,...]}} = items held (AND) and/or rooms visited (AND) to cross the edge to that destination — "requiresRoom" is how an auto-derived story-unlock door (type 1/3, opened by a SetFlag(9,..) event elsewhere) records the producer room (extract_logic.py); "items": {"<itemIdHex>": {"requires": [itemId,...]}} = items held before the pickup of that id in this room is reachable (AND). Item keys are the pickup's id (robust across rebuilds, like staticTargets' destination SSRR), applied to every matching record in the room. OR stays structural + the existing door-type key-set. Provenance for each authored gate lives in data/dc1/map-requirements.md (lock axis: type-1/3 doors, auto-generated by extract_logic.py) and data/dc1/placement-gates.md (placement axis: type-0 script-gated doors, e.g. 0400->0401 / 050C->050D, audited by door_catalog.py --audit). DoorMap.Parse ignores these keys (door-rando only); MapRequirements owns them.
_derivation.itemPriorities: Per-item randomization priority (ITEM-RANDO-PLAN.md §7.1), parsed by MapRequirements and stamped onto NodeItem.Priority; authored for spots that must not move (e.g. the Plug / DDK Input Disc and runtime-armed slots). Per-room optional key: "itemPriorities": [ { "at": "X,Z", "priority": "Fixed" } ] where "at" is the placement quad's first corner (decimal X,Z of the 0x28 record at +0x04/+0x06) and "priority" is Fixed (the only non-Normal value today). Fixed = the pickup stays exactly vanilla (never rerolled or pool-placed), the per-item form of GameDefinition.ItemProtectedRoomCodes; keyed by POSITION (not item id) so one of several same-id spots can be pinned. BioRand's Low/Hidden affect key placement only (KeyItemPlacer) and are deferred. GENERATED for the 22 item_control rooms by scripts/gen_item_map.py from the decoded item gates (room-data.json item_control + per-record pos) — do NOT hand-edit those arrays; run `scripts/gen_item_map.py --apply` and the trigger-decode-contract CI enforces sync. Policy: a pickup is pinned Fixed iff it is runtime-armed (0xff), flag-gated, has an unresolved trigger, or is a relocation twin (same id in >1 record). See docs/dc1/TRIGGER-DECODE.md.
_derivation.cutsceneStatic: Door edges incident to a scripted/cutscene room (GameDefinition.CutsceneRoomCodes: 010D,0112,0109,030A,030E — since 2026-07-12 loaded from data/dc1/cutscene-rooms.json 'cutscene' tier via Dc1RoomExclusions, values unchanged) are tagged static so the shuffle never re-points a FORCED traversal — e.g. the prologue forces Regina through the 0112<->010d door, which is a real 0x28 record (CE-traced 2026-06-20, STATIC-SCD-RE.md cont.24/25); re-pointing it would break the intro. IsStaticEdge is symmetric, so each cutscene room lists its decoded neighbours (out+in), excluding demo-stage (0Axx) targets. EXCEPTION: 010D is the connector's start/growth anchor, so only its forced edge (0112) is locked; its ordinary doors stay shuffleable or the connector cannot grow from start. A full per-script forced-door audit of the remaining cutscene rooms is Increment-C work.
_derivation.transportLinksExcluded: The §6.1 non-door transport/elevator links (no 0x28 record, so never in the door graph): 0401<->060d, 0401<->0614, 0606/0906<->0401, 0509<->050f. Listed for the record; nothing to tag because they are not shuffleable door edges.
_derivation.regions: Sub-room REGION schema for intra-room laser-fence partitions (docs/decisions/dc1/doors/REGION-SCHEMA-PLAN.md; STATIC-SCD-RE cont.62). A fence splits one room into logic sub-regions so a crossing gate binds to the physical sub-region doorway instead of floating to whatever edge points at a destination room (the door-rando gate-floating bug). Optional per-room key: "regions": {"<name>": {"doors": [<destSSRR>,...], "items": ["X,Z",...], "accessFrom": {"<srcRegion>": {"requiresRoom": [SSRR,...], "requires": [itemId,...]}}, "_why": "..."}}. Each region OWNS the external door destinations physically reachable from it (every real door of the room appears in exactly one region) and, for 0606, the pickup positions in its segment (same "X,Z" convention as itemPriorities). accessFrom = intra-room edges keyed by source-region -> the Requirement (AND) to cross the partition, reusing the same requiresRoom/requires semantics as door gates. The PRIMARY (entry) region is the one with NO accessFrom; an incoming external door lands in the region that owns the reciprocal door. An EMPTY accessFrom rule ({}) = always-open (self-enabling / collocated-with-a-typed-door / init-forced-down fences that never block monotonically; see each _why). A room with NO "regions" key is atomic and parses byte-identically to before (regression-critical). All 14 fences / 11 rooms are modelled; only 0102 (2 fences), 010A (door gate) and 0606 (item gate) carry a non-empty rule -- the rest are documented no-ops. Consumers: MapRequirements.ApplyTo stamps each accessFrom rule onto the fence's door edges keyed by the DOORWAY (DoorRecord.OriginalTargetCode, the vanilla dest -- immutable through door-rando's Repoint, so the gate travels with the door instead of floating) and onto 0606's behind pickups (NodeItem.Requires); no graph nodes are split (the reachability engine stays room-code-keyed). gen_ap_logic emits the fence gates as room-granular edges; other map.json readers ignore the key.

shuttle._why: Boarding / 0113-elevator side (0113/050B/0604 doors all at the single boarding point (912,-4088), placed in event sub32, floor-selected by 2:5; 0306 = Main Hallway B1, DDK-L gated). Reachable only by entering 0309 from a shuttle-side door — the deep B2/B3 hub must be reached through the large elevator (heliport route) or the 0113 elevator, NOT the generator stairs. No accessFrom = the west↔shuttle on-foot crossing is [uncertain — needs CE], modeled as blocked (tightest safe model; vanilla still beatable via the elevator/large-elevator). cont.64 §E.

behind._why: laser fence 0:112 (wall z=-1000) is facility-lockdown state (non-monotonic re-lock; the 030C dest stays reachable from the main-side 030C door, so no whole edge is blocked) -> not gated. cont.62
